title: Golf balls: what to play and why it matters less than you think
microFact: At 110, play a soft 2-piece or 3-piece ball around ₹200/ball — losing a ₹450 Pro V1 in a lake hurts twice.
lesson:
Ball construction, decoded:

• 2-PIECE (Surlyn/ionomer cover): durable, low spin, distance-focused, cheap. Callaway Supersoft, Srixon AD333, Titleist TruFeel.
• 3+ PIECE URETHANE cover: soft feel, high greenside spin, premium price. Pro V1, TP5, Chrome Soft.

At your swing stage, greenside spin barely changes your outcomes, but LOST BALLS change your wallet — beginners lose 3–6 per round. The intelligent play everyone respects: a quality mid-tier ball (Srixon AD333 / Q-Star, Supersoft) and — crucially — the SAME model every round. Consistency of ball beats quality of ball, because your distances and feel stay calibrated.

Two insider habits: (1) mark your ball with a unique dot pattern in Sharpie and ANNOUNCE it on the first tee — "Srixon 3, two blue dots" — because playing a wrong ball is a penalty; (2) never tee up a scuffed range ball at a nice club. That's the tell of tells.

quiz.question: Why announce your ball's make and markings on the first tee?
options: So others can lend you an identical ball
Playing a wrong ball is a penalty — identification prevents it
quiz.explanation: Hitting someone else's ball = two-stroke penalty in stroke play. Announcing make, number and markings prevents mix-ups.
